title: Crispy Filipino Quinoa Pancakes
description: Sweet, crunchy pancakes made with quinoa, pumpkin (dum) flavor, perfect for a brunch mood Filipino brunch.
instructions: Rinse quinoa and cook in 2 cups water until fluffy, about 15 minutes; allow to cool.
Beat eggs, milk, vanilla, brown sugar, and baking powder until combined.
Stir in melted coconut oil, then fold in the cooled quinoa until evenly mixed.
Add toasted sesame seeds and mix gently.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and lightly oil the surface with a few drops of coconut oil.
Pour batter to form pancakes about 3–4 inches in diameter; cook until edges set, about 2–3 minutes.
Flip the pancakes and cook until the other side is golden and crispy, about 2–3 minutes more.
Repeat with remaining batter, adding oil as needed to keep pancakes from sticking.
Serve warm, drizzled with honey, and optionally topped with sliced mango or banana.
Garnish with a sprinkle of shredded coconut if desired.
